full_prompt: Design the airframe and internal structure of a university-student rocket for a national student-rocket launch benchmark. Gross lift-off weight (GLOW) must be 9 kg with a tolerance of plus or minus 500 g (8.5 kg to 9.5 kg total). Apogee altitude is hard-capped at 400 m above launch rail height — exceeding 400 m is disqualification. Total propellant mass must not exceed 400 g. Static stability margin (CG-to-CP separation) must be between 1.0 and 2.5 calibers (one caliber equals one body-tube outside diameter). Ammonium perchlorate and potassium perchlorate oxidizers are explicitly prohibited per the rulebook safety rules; allowable propellants are typically KNSB (potassium nitrate / sorbitol) or commercially certified solid motors not on the banned list. Body tube ID is selected to accommodate the team's internal electronics bay and recovery system; this prompt targets 98 mm ID, total length approximately 1.8 m, and a phenolic or carbon-wrapped body tube. The airframe must survive (a) peak motor thrust phase with 12 g axial acceleration (equivalent quasi-static), (b) 8 g lateral gust, and (c) 10 g recovery deployment shock. Internal bulkheads must not deflect more than 1.5 mm under the 12 g axial load. The fin root attachment must survive a 75 N side force at the fin tip. A linear buckling eigenvalue on the body tube under 12 g axial must yield a load factor of at least 1.5. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static + linear buckling) plus a stability-margin check via Barrowman / OpenRocket (non-FEA). Propellant mass and altitude cap are compliance checks tied to propellant data sheets and OpenRocket simulation output. Vibration, thermal, fluid (CFD), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the airframe + internal bays, a mass budget, and a report listing GLOW in kg, propellant mass in g, stability caliber, body tube buckling load factor at 12 g, and predicted apogee in m.
short_prompt:
Design the airframe and internal structure of a university-student rocket for a national student-rocket launch benchmark. GLOW 9 kg +/- 500 g; apogee hard-capped at 400 m above launch-rail height (exceeding 400 m disqualifies); total propellant mass <= 400 g. Allowed propellants exclude ammonium perchlorate and potassium perchlorate (rulebook safety list; KNSB or certified non-banned solid motors acceptable - propellant-sheet compliance check). Static stability margin between 1.0 and 2.5 calibers (Barrowman / OpenRocket). Body-tube ID 98 mm, total length ~1.8 m, phenolic or carbon-wrapped body tube. Body-tube linear buckling under the 12 g axial must have load factor >= 1.5.

Load cases.
- LC1 motor-burn axial (equivalent quasi-static): 12 G; bulkhead deflection <= 1.5 mm.
- LC2 lateral gust: 8 G.
- LC3 recovery deployment shock: 10 G axial.
- LC4 fin-tip side load: 75 N at the fin tip.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Stability caliber and apogee are computed from Barrowman / OpenRocket, not FEA.
Banned oxidizer list is a catalog lookup.

prompt.title: University-student rocket airframe
prompt.brief: Design a ~9 kg GLOW rocket, apogee ≤ 400 m, propellant ≤ 400 g, 1-2.5 caliber stability, no ammonium/potassium perchlorate, 12 g axial survival.
